title: Angraecum dauphinense
taxonomy: Angraecum dauphinense is a species of orchid in the genus Angraecum, which falls under the family Orchidaceae. This classification is based on its physical characteristics and evolutionary relationships with other orchids. The Orchidaceae family is one of the largest and most diverse families of flowering plants. Angraecum dauphinense is further grouped into the subfamily Epidendroideae, which contains many of the showy orchids. The specific classification of Angraecum dauphinense highlights its unique position within the orchid family.

In traditional medicine, Angraecum dauphinense has been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and other inflammatory-related ailments.
